proposed_from.topic: Sharp efficiency-equivalence/non-equivalence theorem across the four leading staggered DiD estimators: Borusyak-Jaravel-Spiess (2024 RES) imputation, Sun-Abraham (2021 JoE) interaction-weighted, Callaway-Sant'Anna (2021 JoE) doubly-robust group-time, and Wooldridge (2021) cohort-time-saturated TWFE. Under heterogeneous treatment effects in cohort and event-time, all four point-identify the same average treatment effect on the treated (ATT) under no-anticipation + parallel trends. The flagship question (axis b, equivalence/non-equivalence frontier between named published estimators): characterize the sharp boundary at which these four estimators have asymptotically equivalent influence functions vs. strictly different ones, as a function of (i) the cohort-time treatment-effect heterogeneity matrix Tau, (ii) the cohort-share vector pi, and (iii) the pre-period covariance matrix Sigma. The kernel claim is a closed-form spectral non-equivalence theorem: the BJS imputation IF and the SA-CS-Wooldridge IFs span identical orthogonal-score subspaces if and only if a low-rank condition rank(Tau) <= K_star holds, where K_star is a sharp threshold equal to the smallest singular value of an explicit observable kernel matrix combining pi and Sigma; when rank(Tau) > K_star, the four estimators have strictly distinct asymptotic variances and BJS achieves the semiparametric efficiency bound while the others sacrifice efficiency proportional to the rank gap. K_star is a new mathematical object computable from observable cohort shares and pre-period covariances, not an existence/absence statement from prior work. Recovers the well-known equivalence under homogeneous effects (rank(Tau)=0) and provides the first sharp efficiency-equivalence boundary for staggered DiD in closed form.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art remains panel_honest_did_phase, which studies cohort-vs-aggregate sensitivity frontiers, and q1_spectral_phase_transition, which studies event-window row-space visibility. Neither proposes a four-estimator BJS/SA/CS/Wooldridge canonical influence-function frontier for the same aggregate ATT; the v5 patch only fixes scaling conventions and does not change that novelty axis.","published_axis":"Closest published papers are BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024, AguilarLoyo2025, RothSantAnna2021Efficient, and ChenSantAnnaXie2025. They cover efficient imputation, TWFE/DiD estimand decomposition, randomized-rollout efficiency, and closed-form modern DiD EIFs, but not the necessary-and-sufficient quotient row-space equality condition across the four named parallel-trends estimators; v5 clarifies that the proposed Gamma_m is a raw loading so the variance formula is well-posed."}
seed_list: Prior work BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024 establishes efficient imputation for staggered event studies. Our seed conjectures that efficiency-equivalence with SA, CS, and Wooldridge holds only on a covariance-whitened active row-space, because heterogeneity directions outside pre-period score span are invisible to their nuisance projections.

Prior work ChenSantAnnaXie2025 establishes closed-form EIFs and overidentification restrictions. Our seed conjectures that extra pre-period moments change the EIF only when their singular directions intersect the ATT heterogeneity row-space, because redundant diagnostics project out before the efficient score is formed.

Prior work CallawaySantAnna2021 separates group-time ATT identification from aggregation. Our seed conjectures that aggregation-equivalent estimators can still have non-equivalent influence functions, because aggregation weights may agree while nuisance projections differ off the target null space.

Prior work SunAbraham2021 shows event-study TWFE contamination and proposes interaction-weighted estimators. Our seed conjectures that contamination-free point identification does not imply efficiency equivalence, because clean cohort-event weighting can discard pre-period covariance directions used by imputation.

Prior work Wooldridge2021 establishes saturated TWFE/Mundlak implementation equivalences. Our seed conjectures that implementation equivalence fails as an IF-equivalence theorem unless the cohort-time saturation row-space contains the BJS active score, because regression algebra can match coefficients while losing efficient nuisance scores.

Prior work SantAnnaZhao2020 derives two-period DiD efficiency bounds. Our seed conjectures that multi-period staggered designs have a new active-rank efficiency frontier absent in two-period DiD, because multiple pre-period covariance directions create score-changing overidentification restrictions.

Prior work RothSantAnnaBilinskiPoe2023 synthesizes unresolved estimator-choice guidance. Our seed conjectures that estimator choice becomes a computable spectral diagnostic, because the same ATT target can have equal or unequal first-order variance depending on the active rank of H(pi,Sigma,a).

Prior work BelleGoBenatiaDortetBernadet2025 develops chained DiD for unbalanced panels. Our seed conjectures that missing pre-period covariance directions shift the efficiency frontier discontinuously, because cohort-specific comparison graphs alter the rank of the observable score kernel.

Prior proposal panel_honest_did_phase flagged conjectural rank-phase and operator-certificate gaps. Our seed conjectures that the certificate becomes economically non-tautological when it classifies IF equivalence across named estimators, because the rejected trust-region-only spectral threshold is tied to an estimator-choice frontier.

Prior proposal q1_spectral_phase_transition proposed singular values of event-window FWL Gram blocks. Our seed conjectures that the same spectral object becomes an efficiency threshold only after covariance whitening by Sigma and ATT aggregation by a, because visibility of effects and equality of influence functions are different row-space questions.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024 establishes efficient imputation for staggered event studies. Our seed conjectures that efficiency-equivalence with SA, CS, and Wooldridge holds only on a covariance-whitened active row-space, because heterogeneity directions outside pre-period score span are invisible to their nuisance projections.

anchor_paper.author: Borusyak, Jaravel, Spiess
anchor_paper.claim: BJS derive an efficient imputation estimator for staggered event-study designs with unrestricted heterogeneity, but do not give a four-estimator IF-equivalence frontier.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/non-equivalence pattern is not blocked: it targets named published estimators and promotes the obstruction to an open row-space class, not a hand-tuned witness.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024 because BJS already derives the efficient estimator.
seed_details.rebuttal: BJS proves efficiency for imputation, not necessary-and-sufficient IF-subspace equality with SA, CS, and Wooldridge for the same ATT aggregation.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ChenSantAnnaXie2025 establishes closed-form EIFs and overidentification restrictions. Our seed conjectures that extra pre-period moments change the EIF only when their singular directions intersect the ATT heterogeneity row-space, because redundant diagnostics project out before the efficient score is formed.

anchor_paper.author: Chen, Sant'Anna, Xie
anchor_paper.claim: Chen, Sant'Anna, and Xie derive closed-form EIFs for DiD and event-study parameters under modern DiD assumptions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift pattern is not blocked: the seed asks when pre-period information changes the efficient score, not merely T=2 to general T.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from ChenSantAnnaXie2025 because closed-form EIFs already encode all pre-period moment restrictions.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their EIF formulas do not classify equality across the four named estimator implementations or isolate the active-rank boundary.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work CallawaySantAnna2021 separates group-time ATT identification from aggregation. Our seed conjectures that aggregation-equivalent estimators can still have non-equivalent influence functions, because aggregation weights may agree while nuisance projections differ off the target null space.

anchor_paper.author: Callaway, Sant'Anna
anchor_paper.claim: Callaway and Sant'Anna identify and aggregate group-time ATTs in multi-period DiD, but do not characterize IF equality with BJS or saturated TWFE.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target-variation pattern is not blocked: the seed reveals an identification-versus-efficiency gap hidden by common ATT aggregation.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from CallawaySantAnna2021 because their group-time ATT influence functions already determine aggregation inference.
seed_details.rebuttal: They determine CS inference, not equality or strict variance separation relative to BJS, SA, and Wooldridge.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work SunAbraham2021 shows event-study TWFE contamination and proposes interaction-weighted estimators. Our seed conjectures that contamination-free point identification does not imply efficiency equivalence, because clean cohort-event weighting can discard pre-period covariance directions used by imputation.

anchor_paper.author: Sun, Abraham
anchor_paper.claim: Sun and Abraham show event-study TWFE contamination under heterogeneity and propose interaction-weighted estimators.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extension pattern is not blocked: the claimed surprise is a point-ID/efficiency split for a named estimator, not applying SA elsewhere.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from SunAbraham2021 because interaction weighting already fixes heterogeneous-effect contamination.
seed_details.rebuttal: The paper fixes contamination of estimands, not semiparametric efficiency or IF equality with imputation and group-time estimators.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Wooldridge2021 establishes saturated TWFE/Mundlak implementation equivalences. Our seed conjectures that implementation equivalence fails as an IF-equivalence theorem unless the cohort-time saturation row-space contains the BJS active score, because regression algebra can match coefficients while losing efficient nuisance scores.

anchor_paper.author: Wooldridge
anchor_paper.claim: Wooldridge establishes TWFE/Mundlak/pooled-OLS equivalences for intervention analysis with staggered entry and heterogeneous effects.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Non-equivalence pattern is not blocked: it targets a named implementation-equivalence claim and asks for an open row-space class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Wooldridge2021 because saturated regression already implements the robust estimand.
seed_details.rebuttal: Implementation equivalence for coefficients is not an IF-subspace equivalence or efficiency-bound statement across all four estimators.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work SantAnnaZhao2020 derives two-period DiD efficiency bounds. Our seed conjectures that multi-period staggered designs have a new active-rank efficiency frontier absent in two-period DiD, because multiple pre-period covariance directions create score-changing overidentification restrictions.

anchor_paper.author: Sant'Anna, Zhao
anchor_paper.claim: Sant'Anna and Zhao derive semiparametric efficiency bounds for ATT in two-period DiD designs.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: T=2 to multi-period pattern is not blocked: the seed asserts a new rank boundary from multiple pre-period score directions, not a routine extension.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from SantAnnaZhao2020 because their semiparametric framework extends to repeated periods.
seed_details.rebuttal: The two-period bound has no staggered cohort-event active-rank object and no four-estimator comparison.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work RothSantAnnaBilinskiPoe2023 synthesizes unresolved estimator-choice guidance. Our seed conjectures that estimator choice becomes a computable spectral diagnostic, because the same ATT target can have equal or unequal first-order variance depending on the active rank of H(pi,Sigma,a).

anchor_paper.author: Roth, Sant'Anna, Bilinski, Poe
anchor_paper.claim: Roth et al. synthesize recent DiD advances and highlight unresolved estimator-choice and implementation issues.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a — orthogonal to blocklist; this seed sharpens a survey-level open problem into a theorem-level diagnostic.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is too practice-oriented and immediate from the survey's recommendations.
seed_details.rebuttal: The survey gives guidance, not a necessary-and-sufficient spectral IF-equivalence theorem.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BelleGoBenatiaDortetBernadet2025 develops chained DiD for unbalanced panels. Our seed conjectures that missing pre-period covariance directions shift the efficiency frontier discontinuously, because cohort-specific comparison graphs alter the rank of the observable score kernel.

anchor_paper.author: Belle'go, Benatia, Dortet-Bernadet
anchor_paper.claim: Belle'go, Benatia, and Dortet-Bernadet develop chained DiD for unbalanced staggered panels and discuss efficiency gains.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assignment-process variation is not blocked: missingness changes rank and comparison-score geometry, not merely balanced to unbalanced panels.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from chained DiD because unbalanced panels are exactly its subject.
seed_details.rebuttal: Chained DiD does not classify equality with BJS, SA, CS, and Wooldridge by a covariance-whitened active rank.

seed_details.one_line: Prior proposal panel_honest_did_phase flagged conjectural rank-phase and operator-certificate gaps. Our seed conjectures that the certificate becomes economically non-tautological when it classifies IF equivalence across named estimators, because the rejected trust-region-only spectral threshold is tied to an estimator-choice frontier.

anchor_paper.author: ThmSmith prior-proposal bank
anchor_paper.claim: Reviewer requested a generic open-set theorem and explicit computable operator certificate rather than a tautological h_agg/h_coh ratio.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Prior-proposal anchor is not blocked: the seed uses the reviewer objection to avoid a bare trust-region spectral threshold and tie the operator to named estimators.
seed_details.referee_objection: This repeats the rejected panel_honest_did_phase spectral threshold because it is still linear algebra.
seed_details.rebuttal: The new kernel classifies published estimator IFs and variance gaps, not support-function sensitivity frontiers.

seed_details.one_line: Prior proposal q1_spectral_phase_transition proposed singular values of event-window FWL Gram blocks. Our seed conjectures that the same spectral object becomes an efficiency threshold only after covariance whitening by Sigma and ATT aggregation by a, because visibility of effects and equality of influence functions are different row-space questions.

anchor_paper.author: ThmSmith prior-proposal bank
anchor_paper.claim: Prior proposal flagged singular values of the event-window FWL Gram block and active nuisance subspace.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens/extends pattern is not blocked: the surprise is that effect visibility and IF equality require different whitened row-space certificates.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from q1_spectral_phase_transition because both are singular-value thresholds.
seed_details.rebuttal: The prior proposal concerns event-window visibility, not semiparametric influence-function equality across named estimators.

proposed_from.literature_map: Borusyak-Jaravel-Spiess (2024, RES, BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024, https://doi.org/10.1093/restud/rdae007) is the efficiency anchor: staggered event-study DiD with heterogeneous effects and efficient imputation. Sun-Abraham (2021, JoE, SunAbraham2021, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2020.09.006) is the contamination anchor: lead/lag TWFE coefficients can load on other-period effects, and interaction-weighted estimators remove that contamination. Callaway-Sant'Anna (2021, JoE, CallawaySantAnna2021, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2020.12.001) is the group-time ATT anchor: identify ATT(g,t), aggregate, and conduct inference. Wooldridge (2021, SSRN, Wooldridge2021, https://doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.3906345) supplies the saturated TWFE/Mundlak implementation route. Goodman-Bacon (2021, JoE) and de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille (2020, AER) explain nonconvex or negative ordinary-TWFE weighting under heterogeneity. Sant'Anna-Zhao (2020, JoE) gives two-period doubly robust DiD efficiency bounds. Chen-Sant'Anna-Xie (2025, arXiv/Cowles, https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.17729) derives closed-form EIFs for modern DiD/event-study parameters and emphasizes overidentification restrictions from multiple pre-periods. Roth-Sant'Anna-Bilinski-Poe (2023, JoE, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2023.03.008) synthesize modern DiD and foreground unresolved estimator-choice issues. Belle'go-Benatia-Dortet-Bernadet (2025, JoE, https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jeconom.2024.105783) extends efficiency questions to unbalanced staggered panels. The live gap is exact first-order equivalence: when named robust estimators have the same influence function, and when distinct nuisance projections produce a strict variance gap.

banked_reason: Stage -0.5 NO-PASS@flagship after 3 angles × 5 revises (15 attempts). NEW failure mode under widened rubric: 14 of 15 versions reached REVISE@flagship (vs. all-REVISE@field before rubric change) — kernel recognized at flagship axis (b) for non-equivalence between BJS imputation, SA, CS, Wooldridge with K_star spectral threshold. Cap=5 revises/angle insufficient to clear well-posedness flags (K_star=0 corner case undefined, Z_M projection loss not uniquely specified across the 4 estimators). Suggests proposer-side issue: nitpicks dominate at flagship tier; either cap should lift conditionally on persistent flagship REVISE, or proposer needs tighter well-posedness scaffolding. Math+novelty sound at flagship; ACCEPT was reachable with 1-2 more revises.
